channel.title: Frontion - Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu's Top Rivals Form Alliance to Challenge His Coalition
item.description: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's most formidable political rivals announced they are joining forces to contest the upcoming election and oust his coalition government.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇺🇦🇷🇺 Trump Confirms Separate Calls with Putin and Zelensky on Ukraine
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he has had "good conversations" with both Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky as de-escalation efforts continue.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Iran and US Stuck in Awkward "No War, No Peace" Limbo
item.description: As diplomatic efforts stall with Trump canceling envoy visits to Pakistan, both Washington and Tehran are betting they can outlast the other in a precarious stalemate without a formal deal.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Iran's Foreign Minister Returns to Pakistan for Key US-Iran Talks
item.description: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi arrived back in Islamabad for high-level meetings as Pakistan seeks to revive peace negotiations between the US and Iran after Trump canceled the dispatch of American envoys.

item.title: 🇨🇴 Colombia bus bombing kills 13 amid escalating drug-trafficking violence
item.description: An explosive device detonated on a bus in Cauca province, killing 13 and injuring 38 including five children. Army chief called it a terrorist act blamed on FARC dissidents, amid a wave of 26 similar incidents in the last two days.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇺🇦 France trains Ukrainian pilots on Mirage 2000-5F fighters under EU mission
item.description: French Air Force pilots are training Ukrainian crews on Mirage 2000-5F aircraft within the EUMAM framework. The training includes combat operations and maintenance, marking a concrete contribution to European security.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Netanyahu warns Hezbollah is dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu accused Hezbollah of undermining the ceasefire agreement with Lebanon, asserting Israel will continue operations against threats. IDF struck Hezbollah operatives and infrastructure in south Lebanon.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Scraps Pakistan Talks
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi returned to Pakistan for diplomatic efforts, but President Trump canceled envoy talks citing no progress. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms, leaving ceasefire prospects dim.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚔️ Trump Likely Target of White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said President Trump and cabinet officials were likely the targets of a gunman who fired on security personnel at the annual White House Correspondents Association dinner. The suspect was taken into custody.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ How the Shooting at White House Correspondents Dinner Unfolded
item.description: A gunman fired shots inside the hotel hosting the White House Correspondents Association dinner, causing President Trump and his cabinet to be evacuated. Security personnel engaged the suspect who was quickly apprehended.

item.title: 🇺🇸📺 White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting Live Updates
item.description: President Trump was safely evacuated from the White House Correspondents Association Dinner after shots were fired at the venue. The suspect is in custody and multiple officials provide ongoing updates.

item.title: 🇺🇸🔍 Suspect in White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting Identified
item.description: The Secret Service identified the suspect as Cole Tomas Allen, armed with a shotgun. He fired on security agents guarding the event before being taken into custody. No injuries to the President or officials were reported.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Armed Man Storms White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump Evacuated Safely
item.description: A 31-year-old man armed with guns and knives charged the Washington Hilton ballroom during the White House Correspondents dinner. President Trump was safely evacuated and one Secret Service officer was injured. The suspect is in custody.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Defense Minister Killed in Coordinated Attack by Al-Qaeda and Tuareg Rebels
item.description: General Sadio Camara, a key figure in Malis military government, was killed during coordinated nationwide attacks by JNIM (al-Qaeda affiliate) and Tuareg fighters. The assault targeted multiple locations including the garrison town of Kati near the capital.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Car Bomb Explodes Outside Northern Ireland Police Station
item.description: A car bomb exploded outside a police station in Dunmurry near Belfast after attackers placed an improvised device in a delivery drivers vehicle. Police condemned the attack as an attempt to undermine the Good Friday Agreement.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli President Delays Decision on Netanyahu Pardon, Pushes for Plea Deal
item.description: President Isaac Herzog said he will delay deciding on Prime Minister Netanyahus pardon request for corruption cases, urging that mediation efforts be exhausted first. The decision comes amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Drone Strikes Kill 5 in Dnipro as Ukraine War Enters Fifth Year
item.description: Russian drone and missile attacks killed at least 5 people and wounded 46 in Dnipro. The strikes destroyed residential buildings and businesses. Romania reported drone fragments on its territory near the Ukraine border.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel sent Iron Dome and troops to UAE during Iran war
item.description: Axios reports Israel deployed an Iron Dome battery and personnel to the UAE amid the Iran conflict, deepening defense ties between the countries.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Tuareg rebels announce Russian Africa Corps withdrawal from Kidal
item.description: Tuareg rebels in northern Mali reached a deal for the Russian Africa Corps to withdraw from Kidal as fighting continues between the army and separatist forces.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: Israeli PM says Hezbollah actions are threatening the ceasefire with Lebanon as sirens warn of suspected drone attacks in northern Israel.

item.title: 🛢️ Russia warns months needed to rebalance oil market after Hormuz blockade
item.description: Russian Deputy PM Alexander Novak warned of deep energy crisis as the Hormuz blockade prevents large volumes of oil from reaching global markets.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China positioned to benefit from energy-driven renewables race
item.description: The US-Iran war may have inadvertently triggered a global energy transition favoring Beijing's green technology leadership, analysts say.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel President Herzog seeks plea deal over pardon for Netanyahu
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's pardon request, instead pushing for mediation that could lead to a plea deal in his corruption trial. Netanyahu faces charges in three cases.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Car bomb explodes outside Belfast police station in suspected dissident attack
item.description: A car bomb exploded outside a police station in Dunmurry, south Belfast, after a delivery vehicle was hijacked. Northern Ireland police called it a "miracle" no one was injured and suspect dissident republicans, possibly the New IRA.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia announces M armored vehicle deal amid weapons buildup
item.description: Australia will spend more than $800 million on armored vehicles, adding to its recent weapons purchases as the Middle East conflict drives concerns about regional security and supply chain vulnerabilities.
